assertions.expectation: Explains that Extended channel provides up to 24 months of support vs 14 months for standard channels
assertions.reason: Response clearly states Extended channel provides 'up to 24 months of support' for Kubernetes versions 1.27+ vs. '14 months on Regular/Stable'

assertions.expectation: Mentions that Extended channel has additional cost (it's not free)
assertions.reason: Response explicitly mentions 'Additional cost: Only during the extended support period (months 15-24). No extra cost during standard support (months 1-14)'

assertions.expectation: Notes that Extended channel gets the same versions as Regular but keeps them available longer
assertions.reason: Response indicates Extended gets same patch timing as Regular and keeps versions available for extended support period, though it could be more explicit about getting the same versions

assertions.expectation: Warns that staying on older versions longer means missing new features and accumulating technical debt
assertions.reason: Response does not explicitly warn about missing new features or accumulating technical debt from staying on older versions longer

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel to Extended
assertions.reason: Response provides the exact command: 'gcloud container clusters update CLUSTER_NAME --zone ZONE --release-channel extended'

assertions.expectation: Advises checking that the current version (1.31) is available on Extended before switching
assertions.reason: Response provides specific command to check if 1.31 is available in Extended channel and offers alternative if it's not available yet

assertions.expectation: Recommends Extended for environments with compliance requirements or slow internal change processes
assertions.reason: Response concludes that Extended channel 'aligns well with production change control processes' and emphasizes control benefits for production environments

assertions.expectation: Mentions that Extended doesn't mean you can skip upgrades forever — you still must upgrade within 24 months
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'Month 24: End of extended support, must upgrade or face forced upgrade' and mentions forced upgrades at end of extended support

assertions.expectation: Notes that minor version upgrades on Extended are NOT automated (except at EoS) — customers must plan and initiate them manually
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'No automatic minor version upgrades: Control plane minor upgrades are manual (except at end of extended support)' and 'Minor upgrades become manual: Plan and trigger them yourself'
